content.text:
Module:
IT Project Management  |
Semester:
2  |
Type:
UET2
VHS:
42h  |
Weekly hours:
3h  |
Lectures:
1h30  |
TD:
-  |
TP:
1h30
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
1  |
Credits:
2
Teaching Objectives
Understand the principles and methods of IT project management
Execute the different phases of an IT project, from design to closure
Acquire skills in planning, organizing, monitoring and controlling projects
Identify and manage risks related to IT projects
Use IT project management tools and techniques
Work as a team in a project management context
Recommended Prerequisites
Software engineering, information systems
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Introduction to IT Project Management
Definition and challenges of IT project management
IT project production process: realization, management, quality processes
Types of IT projects (software development, infrastructure, etc.)
Stakeholders in an IT project
Project Management Methodologies
Traditional methodologies (V-cycle)
Agile methodologies (Scrum, Kanban)
Planning and Estimation
Scope and objectives definition
Work breakdown structure (WBS)
Scheduling (Gantt chart, PERT)
Resource estimation (time, budget, personnel, costs, COCOMO)
Planning tools (Microsoft Project, Asana, etc.)
Risk Management
Risk identification and analysis (SWOT matrix, Ishikawa diagram)
Probability and impact assessment
Monitoring and Control
Project progress tracking
Change management
Communication and reporting
Dashboards and KPIs
Resource Management
Identifying required resources
Resource allocation to tasks
Conflict and priority management
Quality Management
Quality criteria definition
Quality control procedures
Testing and validation
Project Closure
Closure documentation
Knowledge and skills transfer
Stakeholder satisfaction assessment
Lessons learned
IT Project Management Tools
Project management software
Knowledge management systems
Communication and collaboration platforms
Assessment
Semester exam (in-person): 60%
Continuous assessment (CC): 40% — continuous tests, personal work
Bibliographic References
Project Management Institute (PMI) (2021). A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK Guide). https://www.pmi.org
Andrew Stellman & Jennifer Greene (2005). Applied Software Project Management. Theory In Practice.
Harold Kerzner (2017). Project Management: A Systems Approach to Planning, Scheduling, and Controlling. Wiley.
Scott Berkun (2008). Making Things Happen: Mastering Project Management. O'Reilly.
Roger S. Pressman & Bruce R. Maxim (2014). Software Engineering: A Practitioner's Approach. McGraw-Hill.
Steve McConnell (2006). Software Estimation: Demystifying the Black Art. Microsoft Press.
Claude Aubry (2022). Scrum: un outil convivial pour une agilité radicale. Editions Dunod.
Stephen H. Kan (2010). Metrics and Models in Software Quality Engineering (2nd Edition). Addison-Wesley Professional.
Linda Westfall (2009). The Certified Software Quality Engineer Handbook. Quality Press.
Murali Chemuturi (2010). Mastering Software Quality Assurance. J. Ross Publishing.
